when_to_use: To talk about things that happened in the past (yesterday, last week, before).
when_to_use_ar: للتحدث عن أشياء صارت في الماضي (أمس، الأسبوع الماضي، قبل).

part_1_was_were.negative: was not = wasn't · were not = weren't
part_1_was_were.question: Was he tired? · Were they at home?

part_2_regular_verbs.rule: Add -ed to the base verb: work → worked, play → played, study → studied

spelling_rules.rule: Short verb (CVC): double last letter + ed

pronunciation_rules.when: After voiceless sounds (k, p, f, s, sh, ch)

common_errors.explanation: Past simple = just the -ed verb. No 'am/is' before it.
Don't combine 'was' with -ed for regular past. Use one or the other.
'go' is irregular (go → went). Regular -ed only works for regular verbs.

important_note: This week introduces ONLY was/were + regular -ed verbs. Irregular verbs (go→went, eat→ate, see→saw) will be covered in Level 1.
quick_rule: Past = was/were (for states) + verb-ed (for actions). Time signals tell you it's past.
quick_rule_ar: الماضي = was/were (للحالات) + فعل+ed (للأفعال). كلمات الوقت تدلّك إنه ماضي.
connect_to_speaking: Tell someone about yesterday: 'Yesterday I was tired. I worked a lot. I studied English. I watched a movie. I played with my kids. It was a good day.'
